title: Operator tooling focus: Grafana, n8n, Bitwarden
goal: Evaluate and integrate Grafana, n8n and Bitwarden only where they strengthen Heim-PC operator observability, bounded automation and secret hygiene without becoming a second control-plane or weakening Grabowski/Bureau/Leitstand boundaries.
completion: Grafana has a documented read-only observability role, a non-conflicting port/route plan and explicit decision whether it complements or replaces any Netdata/Grafana-lite need.
n8n has a no-authority contract: notifications/proposals only, no queue truth, no PR merge, no deploy and no privileged host mutation.
Bitwarden CLI usage is documented as a fail-closed secret reference path with no secret export into repos, logs, evidence packets or broad agent context.

metadata.source: User request on 2026-07-08: grafana, n8n, bitwarden ins visier nehmen
live_findings: grafana-server and n8n not present in PATH during live check
port 3000 is already listening on heim-pc; Grafana default port would conflict
Leitstand and rLens are already active; new tooling must not become a second operator truth
